# Google Just Put NotebookLM Inside Gemini: Here’s What You Can Do Now

3:00 pm April 11, 2026 By [Julian Horsey](https://www.geeky-gadgets.com/author/jhorsey/)

![Image 1: B2B sales prospecting setup using NotebookLM sources to draft tailored outreach messages inside Gemini chat.](https://www.geeky-gadgets.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/04/notebooklm-gemini-unified-interface.webp)

Google’s integration of **NotebookLM** with the **Gemini AI platform** introduces a structured way to handle information and streamline tasks. As explained by Universe of AI, one standout feature is **persistent memory**, which allows the system to remember details from previous interactions. This is particularly useful for long-term projects, as it reduces repetitive data entry and helps maintain continuity in workflows.

Explore how this update enhances **workflow organization** with features such as task categorization through folders and the ability to consolidate conversations into notebooks. Learn how customizable AI instructions can assist with tasks like summarizing complex research or creating detailed reports. Gain insight into **AI Studio**, which offers tools for organizing knowledge, including mind mapping, flashcard creation and slide deck development.

## Key Features of the NotebookLM & Gemini Integration

**TL;DR Key Takeaways :**

https://www.technologyreview.com/2026/04.title: Want to understand the current state of AI? Check out these charts.
https://www.technologyreview.com/2026/04.text:
URL Source: https://www.technologyreview.com/2026/04/13/1135675/want-to-understand-the-current-state-of-ai-check-out-these-charts/

Published Time: 2026-04-13T09:00:00-04:00

Markdown Content:
If you’re following AI news, you’re probably getting whiplash. AI is a gold rush. AI is a bubble. AI is taking your job. AI can’t even read a clock. The 2026 AI Index from Stanford University’s Institute for Human-Centered Artificial Intelligence, AI’s annual report card, comes out today and cuts through some of that noise.

Despite predictions that AI development may hit a wall, the report says that the top models just keep getting better. People are adopting AI faster than they picked up the personal computer or the internet. AI companies are generating revenue faster than companies in any previous technology boom, but they’re also spending hundreds of billions of dollars on data centers and chips. The benchmarks designed to measure AI, the policies meant to govern it, and the job market are struggling to keep up. AI is sprinting, and the rest of us are trying to find our shoes.

All that speed comes at a cost. AI data centers around the world can now draw 29.6 gigawatts of power, enough to run the entire state of New York at peak demand. Annual water use from running OpenAI’s GPT-4o alone may exceed the drinking water needs of 12 million people. At the same time, the supply chain for chips is alarmingly fragile. The US hosts most of the world’s AI data centers, and one company in Taiwan, TSMC, fabricates almost every leading AI chip.

The data reveals a technology evolving faster than we can manage. Here’s a look at some of the key points from this year’s report.

### **The US and China are nearly tied**

In a long, heated race with immense geopolitical stakes, the US and China are almost neck and neck on AI model performance, according to Arena, a community-driven [ranking platform](https://arena.ai/) that allows users to compare the outputs of large language models on identical prompts. In early 2023, OpenAI had a lead with ChatGPT, but this gap narrowed in 2024 as Google and Anthropic released their own models. In February 2025, R1, an AI model built by the Chinese lab DeepSeek, briefly matched the top US model, ChatGPT. As of March 2026, Anthropic leads, trailed closely by xAI, Google, and OpenAI. Chinese models like DeepSeek and Alibaba lag only modestly. With the best AI models separated in the rankings by razor-thin margins, they’re now competing on cost, reliability, and real-world usefulness.

https://huggingface.co/blog/huggingface/.title: State of Open Source on Hugging Face: Spring 2026
https://huggingface.co/blog/huggingface/.text:
URL Source: https://huggingface.co/blog/huggingface/state-of-os-hf-spring-2026

Published Time: 2026-03-17T16:37:55.002Z

Markdown Content:
[Back to Articles](https://huggingface.co/blog)

*   [**Open Source in Competition**](https://huggingface.co/blog/huggingface/state-of-os-hf-spring-2026#open-source-in-competition "Open Source in Competition")

*   [**The Geography of Open Source**](https://huggingface.co/blog/huggingface/state-of-os-hf-spring-2026#the-geography-of-open-source "The Geography of Open Source")

*   [**Model Popularity**](https://huggingface.co/blog/huggingface/state-of-os-hf-spring-2026#model-popularity "Model Popularity")

*   [**Papers and Scientific Contributions**](https://huggingface.co/blog/huggingface/state-of-os-hf-spring-2026#papers-and-scientific-contributions "Papers and Scientific Contributions")

*   [**Derivative Models**](https://huggingface.co/blog/huggingface/state-of-os-hf-spring-2026#derivative-models "Derivative Models")

*   [**Adoption and Accessibility**](https://huggingface.co/blog/huggingface/state-of-os-hf-spring-2026#adoption-and-accessibility "Adoption and Accessibility")

*   [**Compute, Hardware, and Open Source**](https://huggingface.co/blog/huggingface/state-of-os-hf-spring-2026#compute-hardware-and-open-source "Compute, Hardware, and Open Source")

*   [**Sub-Communities: Robotics**](https://huggingface.co/blog/huggingface/state-of-os-hf-spring-2026#sub-communities-robotics "Sub-Communities: Robotics")

*   [**Sub-Communities: AI for Science**](https://huggingface.co/blog/huggingface/state-of-os-hf-spring-2026#sub-communities-ai-for-science "Sub-Communities: AI for Science")

*   [**Looking Forward**](https://huggingface.co/blog/huggingface/state-of-os-hf-spring-2026#looking-forward "Looking Forward")

This post examines how the open source AI landscape has shifted across competition, geography, technical trends, and emerging communities over the past year. We primarily examine community activity on Hugging Face across many types of metrics to give a holistic view of the ecosystem.

This post builds on an earlier analysis conducted mid-2025, [available here](https://huggingface.co/blog/evijit/hf-hub-ecosystem-overview), which examined what the Hugging Face Community is building. We recommend reading additional perspectives on the open source ecosystem in and outside of Hugging Face from the [Data Provenance Initiative](https://arxiv.org/abs/2512.03073), [Interconnects](https://www.interconnects.ai/), [OpenRouter and a16z](https://openrouter.ai/state-of-ai), and [MIT and the Linux Foundation](https://www.linuxfoundation.org/blog/revealing-the-hidden-economics-of-open-models-in-the-ai-era). As the Hugging Face ecosystem is distributed, analyses are a combination of Hugging Face and community members' work, each of which is appropriately credited.

![Image 3: OpenAI logo](https://releasebot.io/api/logo/471?domain=github.com&source=https%3A%2F%2Fgithub.com%2Fopenai%2Fcodex%2Freleases%3Fq%3Dprerelease%253Afalse%26expanded%3Dtrue)

[Codex](https://releasebot.io/updates/openai/codex) by [OpenAI](https://releasebot.io/updates/openai)

## 0.120.0

Codex adds Realtime V2 background agent streaming, clearer TUI hook status and custom status lines, plus more precise MCP tool typing. It also fixes Windows sandbox, remote TLS, tool ordering, and MCP cleanup issues while updating docs and analytics.

### New Features

    *   Realtime V2 can now stream background agent progress while work is still running and queue follow-up responses until the active response completes (#17264, #17306)
    *   Hook activity in the TUI is easier to scan, with live running hooks shown separately and completed hook output kept only when useful (#17266)
    *   Custom TUI status lines can include the renamed thread title (#17187)
    *   Code-mode tool declarations now include MCP outputSchema details so structured tool results are typed more precisely (#17210)
    *   SessionStart hooks can distinguish sessions created by /clear from fresh startup or resume sessions (#17073)

### Bug Fixes

https://huggingface.co/blog/multimodal-s.title: Multimodal Embedding & Reranker Models with Sentence Transformers
https://huggingface.co/blog/multimodal-s.text:
URL Source: https://huggingface.co/blog/multimodal-sentence-transformers

Published Time: 2026-04-09T00:00:00.686Z

Markdown Content:
[Back to Articles](https://huggingface.co/blog)

[Sentence Transformers](https://sbert.net/) is a Python library for using and training embedding and reranker models for applications like retrieval augmented generation, semantic search, and more. With the v5.4 update, you can now **encode and compare texts, images, audio, and videos** using the same familiar API. In this blogpost, I'll show you how to use these new multimodal capabilities for both embedding and reranking.

Multimodal embedding models map inputs from different modalities into a shared embedding space, while multimodal reranker models score the relevance of mixed-modality pairs. This opens up use cases like visual document retrieval, cross-modal search, and multimodal RAG pipelines.
